seo.page_title: Toyota Noah vs Voxy 2020 – import to NZ
seo.meta_description: Compare 2020 Toyota Noah vs Voxy for NZ import. Seating, comfort, features and auction buying factors, with compliance as part of the process.
seo.h1_tag: Toyota Noah vs Voxy 2020: which suits your NZ life?
seo.og_title: Toyota Noah vs Voxy 2020 – NZ import comparison
seo.og_description: Side‑by‑side look at 2020 Toyota Noah and Voxy for NZ buyers. Seating, comfort, features, auction grades and what to check before importing.
seo.primary_keyword: Toyota Noah vs Voxy 2020 import NZ

blocks.hero_h1: Toyota Noah vs Voxy 2020: which suits your NZ life?
blocks.hero_subtitle: Comparing the 2020 Toyota Noah and Toyota Voxy for NZ import buyers, with a focus on space, comfort and day‑to‑day running costs.
proof_points: Japan specialists with direct access to major auctions
Importing Estimate — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The 2020 Toyota Noah and Voxy are popular with NZ families and community groups who need seven or eight seats without going to a full‑size van. Sliding doors make school runs and tight car parks easier, and the tall cabin suits child seats and taller adults. Petrol use is typically reasonable for the size, especially on open‑road trips.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes — from Japan auction to NZ road‑ready
savings_block.body: With 2020 Noah and Voxy models, the total landed cost typically starts with the hammer price at Japanese auction, then adds shipping, marine insurance and NZ port charges. On top of that, you factor in NZ entry certification, compliance work, registration, on‑road costs and our service fee. We lay out each of these items up front so you can see how the final NZ landed cost is built from the original auction price.

cta_form.headline: Find your Noah or Voxy — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Here’s a typical 2020 Toyota Noah/Voxy cabin layout, showing the sliding doors, low step‑in height and foldable middle row that make school runs and airport trips easier.
blocks.main_usp_deep: Both the 2020 Toyota Noah and Voxy share the same basic platform, so you’re choosing mainly on styling, interior trim and feature levels rather than mechanical differences. That makes them easier to shop for at Japanese auctions, because most parts and servicing needs are shared. For NZ buyers, the big draw is flexible seating that can handle kids, grandparents and luggage without feeling like a commercial van. If documentation is clean and mileage is sensible, they typically meet NZ entry standards with routine work only.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You’ve got a growing family, a busy community role, or you’re running airport runs and rideshare work where easy access and comfort matter more than badge prestige. You want something that’s easy to park, can handle weekend trips, and doesn’t feel too thirsty for its size. For that mix of people‑moving and everyday use, a 2020 Noah or Voxy is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’re an NZ-based team that’s been importing vehicles from Japan since 2009, with hundreds of people movers sourced over that time. We work directly with the major auction houses, so you see the same auction sheets we do before any bidding happens. Our role is to explain the grades, note any modifications or previous repairs, and give you a clear landed-cost estimate. Compliance is part of the process, and we stay involved right through to handover in NZ.

cta_mid_page.headline: Tell us whether you prefer Noah or Voxy
cta_mid_page.subtext: Share your seating needs, budget and preferred look, and we’ll shortlist real auction cars that match.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how the 2020 Toyota Noah and Voxy typically compare for NZ import buyers.

rows.notes: This comparison focuses on 2020 models, which usually meet current NZ entry standards when documentation is clean. Electronic stability control is standard by this point, which is important for entry certification.

Both 2020 Noah and Voxy are usually petrol people movers with similar outputs and automatic gearboxes. Real‑world differences in performance and fuel use are minor, so condition and maintenance history matter more than badge choice.

Noah trims tend to lean towards a more conservative family look, while Voxy trims often have sportier bumpers, darker interiors and different wheel designs. Higher grades on both can add features like power sliding doors, upgraded infotainment and extra safety tech.

Based on auction documentation, these 2020 models usually meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no major modifications are noted. Any aftermarket suspension, body kits or lighting listed on the sheet may need extra checks at entry certification.

Noah and Voxy are both common in Japanese auctions, which typically gives a steady flow of options across different grades and mileages. Popular colours and higher trims can attract more bidding competition and take a little longer to secure.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm whether you’re leaning towards a Noah, a Voxy or either, and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others for suitable 2020 Noah and Voxy listings. For these people movers, we pay close attention to seating layout, auction grade, mileage and any notes about previous use such as private or commercial.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet, photos and our comments on condition and features. Once you're happy with a specific car, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For particular colours or higher‑spec trims, allow a little extra time to secure the right car before it ships. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it’s through, we arrange delivery or you can collect, and you get copies of the original auction documentation for your records.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2020 Noah or Voxy is straightforward, but it helps to understand a few key factors before you start.

items.risk: Worry about buying a high‑use or ex‑commercial people mover
items.solution: We look for clues in the auction sheet such as seat wear, interior condition and use notes, and typically steer buyers towards lower‑wear examples where budget allows. You see the same documentation we do before any bidding.

items.risk: Concern about hidden costs on top of the auction price
items.solution: We break down the landed cost into auction price, shipping, insurance, port charges, entry certification, compliance work, registration and our fee. That way you can see where each dollar goes before you commit.

items.risk: Uncertainty about what auction grades actually mean
items.solution: We explain the grading system in plain language, highlight any repair or paint notes, and point out where a lower grade might still be fine for your needs. You decide what you’re comfortable with based on clear information.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: Tell us whether you’re open to either a Noah or Voxy, your budget, and how many seats you need. We then watch the Japanese auctions, shortlist suitable cars and talk you through the pros and cons of each option. When you’re ready, we handle the bidding, shipping and NZ side so you can focus on choosing the right vehicle.

faq_block.q: Is the 2020 Toyota Noah or Voxy better for a NZ family?
faq_block.a: For most families, they’re very similar in space and practicality. The choice usually comes down to which look you prefer and which trim level you can find within your budget. We can show you real auction examples of both so you can compare features.

faq_block.q: How much does it typically cost to import a 2020 Noah or Voxy?
faq_block.a: Total cost depends on auction price, mileage, grade and trim level. On top of the hammer price you add shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work, registration and our fee. We provide an estimated landed range before you decide to bid.

faq_block.q: Do 2020 Noah and Voxy models usually meet NZ entry standards?
faq_block.a: usually, 2020 examples with clean documentation and no major modifications typically meet NZ entry standards. Final approval is always decided at NZ entry certification after physical inspection. We flag any potential issues we see on the auction sheet before bidding.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving in NZ?
faq_block.a: From a successful bid, shipping to NZ is typically 2–4 weeks depending on sailings. Entry certification, compliance and registration usually add another couple of weeks, depending on workshop schedules. We keep you updated at each stage.

faq_block.q: What auction grade should I look for on a 2020 Noah or Voxy?
faq_block.a: Many buyers aim for mid‑to‑high grades where condition and price balance out, but it depends on your budget and how fussy you are about cosmetics. We explain what each grade and panel note means and share the original auction sheet so you can make an informed call.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to compare real Noah and Voxy options?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us your budget, seating needs and whether you lean Noah or Voxy, and we’ll come back with live Japanese auction options and clear landed pricing.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on fresh 2020 Noah and Voxy listings, plus plain‑English guides to importing from Japan.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based import specialist working directly with Japanese auctions since 2009. We focus on matching buyers with the right vehicles, not just the nearest one. Our role is to explain the documentation, outline the landed costs and manage the process through to NZ compliance.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: When we talk about landed cost for a 2020 Noah or Voxy, we mean everything needed to get the vehicle on the road in NZ, apart from fuel and insurance.

rows.notes: The hammer price you pay at the Japanese auction, based on the specific Noah or Voxy you choose.
Ocean freight from Japan to NZ plus marine insurance to cover the vehicle while it’s in transit.
NZ port charges, entry certification inspection, any required compliance work, registration and standard on‑road costs.

auction_checklist.check: Auction grade and interior/exterior condition notes
auction_checklist.why: Gives a quick view of how the 2020 Noah or Voxy has been treated and whether any repairs or repainting are noted.

auction_checklist.check: Mileage and service stickers or records in photos
auction_checklist.why: Helps you judge typical use and whether servicing appears to have been done regularly.

auction_checklist.check: Seat layout, seat fabric condition and sliding door operation
auction_checklist.why: Important for families and shuttle use, as worn seats or damaged door mechanisms can add to your costs in NZ.
Uneven tyre wear or suspension comments can hint at alignment or suspension work that may be needed after arrival.

auction_checklist.check: Any mention of aftermarket parts or accessories
auction_checklist.why: Non‑factory items like suspension, lighting or body kits may need extra attention at NZ entry certification.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You want a small hatchback or compact SUV with minimal parking footprint.
You need a heavy‑duty tow vehicle for large boats or caravans rather than a people mover.
You’re set on a brand‑new NZ‑new vehicle with local dealer warranty rather than a used Japanese import.

alternatives_block.intro: If a 2020 Noah or Voxy doesn’t quite match what you’re after, there are other Japanese people movers to consider.

rows.better_if: You like the Noah/Voxy size but prefer a more upmarket look and trim feel.
rows.tradeoff: Often costs more at auction for similar year and mileage, and availability can be a bit tighter.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain checks for Noah and Voxy buyers
engine_drivetrain_block.body: The 2020 Toyota Noah and Voxy typically share similar petrol engines and automatic gearboxes, so the main focus is on how each individual vehicle has been used and maintained. At auction we look for signs of regular servicing, clean engine bays and any comments about oil leaks or drivetrain noise. In NZ, most workshops are familiar with Toyota people movers, which usually keeps ongoing maintenance straightforward.
checklist: Confirm engine size and code from the auction sheet so you know exactly what you’re buying.
Check for service stickers or records in the photos to gauge how often oil changes have been done.
Look for any auction notes about oil leaks, engine noise or transmission issues.
Review underbody photos for signs of corrosion or damage that could affect long‑term reliability.
Ask about typical NZ servicing costs for this engine and gearbox so you can budget ahead.
